2606.23165.title: The Language Blind Spot: How Query Language and Brand Recognition Tier Shape AI-Constructed Brand Reputation Across Twelve European Languages
2606.23165.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly mediate how people form impressions of organisations, yet most monitoring is done in English, assuming an English query returns a representative picture. We measure how far that holds. We queried three grounded LLMs (GPT-5.4, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Perplexity Sonar Pro) about 66 brands from eleven Northern, Baltic, and Central European markets, in twelve languages across four families (Germanic, Uralic, Baltic, Slavic), generating 35,640 responses. Multilingual embeddings (BGE-M3) allow cross-language comparison without translation. Three results emerge. First, AI-constructed reputation is language-bound: mean cross-language cosine similarity is 0.825, same-family responses are more similar than cross-family (0.844 vs 0.820; d = 0.31), and sentiment varies by language (F = 268.5, eta^2 = 0.077), with Uralic and Baltic languages most positive and Germanic, including English, most critical; clustering recovers the Slavic and Baltic families (cophenetic 0.915). Second, query language shifts which brands are recommended far more than how they are described: moving from an English query to a brand's home language raises recommendation share by 0.80 for local champions but only 0.15 for global multinationals (t = -8.84, p < 0.001), with no comparable reversal in sentiment. An English-only audit therefore understates a local champion's AI visibility. Third, response stability varies more with model choice than with language (eta^2_model = 0.32 vs eta^2_language = 0.01, on a five-iteration replication over a 20-brand subset). These results indicate that English-only AI reputation monitoring leaves a measurable language blind spot, concentrated in the visibility of locally headquartered brands.
2606.23165.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地影響人們對組織的印象形成，但大多數監測工作都是用英語進行，假設英語查詢能返回一個具有代表性的畫面。 我們測量了這一假設的有效性。 我們對來自十一個北歐、波羅的海和中歐市場的66個品牌進行了查詢，使用了三個有根據的LLM（GPT-5.4、Gemini 3.1 Pro、Perplexity Sonar Pro），涵蓋了十二種語言，屬於四個語言家族（德語系、烏拉爾語系、波羅的海語系、斯拉夫語系），生成了35,640個回應。 多語言嵌入（BGE-M3）允許在不進行翻譯的情況下進行跨語言比較。 三個結果顯現出來。 首先，AI構建的聲譽受語言限制：跨語言的平均餘弦相似度為0.825，同一家族的回應比跨家族的回應更相似（0.844對0.820；d = 0.31），情感因語言而異（F = 268.5，eta^2 = 0.077），烏拉爾語和波羅的海語言的情感最為正面，而德語系，包括英語，則最為批判；聚類恢復了斯拉夫和波羅的海語系（cophenetic 0.915）。 第二，查詢語言的變化對推薦品牌的影響遠大於描述品牌的方式：從英語查詢轉向品牌的母語，當地冠軍的推薦份額提高了0.80，而全球跨國公司的推薦份額僅提高0.15（t = -8.84，p < 0.001），情感上並沒有可比的反轉。因此，僅用英語進行的審計低估了當地冠軍的AI能見度。 第三，回應的穩定性與模型選擇的變化比與語言的變化更大（eta^2_model = 0.32對eta^2_language = 0.01，在20個品牌子集上的五次重複實驗中）。 這些結果表明，僅用英語的AI聲譽監測留下了可測量的語言盲點，集中在當地總部品牌的能見度上。
2606.23165.author: Dmitrij Żatuchin et.al.
